para.dtcname: Vehicle Speed Signal Circuit between Stereo Component Amplifier and Combination Meter

content5.ptxt: This circuit is necessary for the ASL (Auto Sound Leveliser) built into the stereo component amplifier. Speed signals are received from the combination meter and used for the ASL.
The ASL function automatically adjusts the sound data in order to enable hearing the clear audio sound even when vehicle noise increases (as vehicle noise increases, the volume is turned up etc.).

item.ptxt: A voltage of 12 V or 5 V is output from each ECU and then input to the combination meter. The signal is changed to a pulse signal at the transistor in the combination meter. Each ECU controls the respective system based on the pulse signal.
If a short occurs in an ECU, all systems in the diagram below will not operate normally.

test1.ptxt: Drive the vehicle and check if the function of the speedometer on the combination meter is normal.

specitem.ptxt: Actual vehicle speed and the speed indicated on the speedometer are the same.

atten4.ptxt: The vehicle speed sensor is functioning normally when the indication on the speedometer is normal.

test1.ptxt: Disconnect the stereo component amplifier connector F19.

test2.ptxt: Jack up either one of the drive wheels.
Move the shift lever to the neutral position.
Measure the voltage between terminal SPD of the wire harness connector and body ground when the drive wheels are turned slowly.

specitem.ptxt: Voltage pulses as shown in the illustration.

testgrp.testtitle: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (COMBINATION METER - STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER)

test1.ptxt: Disconnect the stereo component amplifier connector F19 and combination meter connector F2.
Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.

testgrp.testtitle: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER - BODY GROUND)

test1.ptxt: Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below.

atten4.ptxt: If the resistance between terminal SPD and body ground is less than 10 kΩ, there may be a short in a wire harness, connector, or an ECU that is connected to the SPD signal wire.

testgrp.testtitle: REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR
REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OR CHECK EACH ECU
